name: AWS WAF Admin Path Deny-by-Default Pack
description: Blocks direct access to common administrative endpoints unless the source IP is explicitly allowlisted via an IP set reference.
objective: Reduce exposure of administrative interfaces by enforcing default deny on high-risk admin paths and allowing access only from approved source networks.
risk_mitigated: Unauthorized probing and direct attack attempts against administrative panels such as /admin, /wp-admin, /phpmyadmin, and /console.
deployment_notes: Start in COUNT mode first to baseline legitimate traffic and identify missing allowlist ranges.
After validating logs and metrics, switch action to BLOCK for enforcement.
Place this rule high in WebACL priority, after explicit trusted allow rules and before generic allow flows.
Ensure the referenced IP set is managed through change control and includes approved office/VPN/jump-host egress ranges.
tuning_notes: Create and maintain an AWS WAF IPSet containing approved source CIDRs for admin access.
During rollout, monitor sampled requests and WAF logs for blocked or counted legitimate admin access.
If your environment uses additional admin paths (e.g., /administrator, /manage), extend the path match list before BLOCK mode.
If behind proxies/CDN, ensure source IP handling is correct (e.g., forwarded IP configuration where applicable) before strict enforcement.
monitoring_guidance: Track rule match counts in CloudWatch metrics (CountedRequests first, then BlockedRequests after cutover).
Alert on spikes in blocked admin-path requests, especially from new source ASNs or geographies.
Review top blocked URI paths and source IPs weekly to validate ongoing efficacy and tune allowlist/path set.
